001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.collections;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
021
022import java.util.*;
023import java.util.function.*;
024
025/**
026 * An {@link ArrayList} that allows you to control whether it's read-only via a constructor parameter.
027 *
028 * <p>
029 * This class provides a unique capability: it can appear as an unmodifiable list to external code (via the standard
030 * {@link List} interface methods) while still allowing internal code to modify it through special "override" methods.
031 * This is useful when you need to pass a list to code that should not modify it, but you still need to modify it
032 * internally.
033 *
034 * <h5 class='section'>Features:</h5>
035 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
036 *    <li><b>Controlled Mutability:</b> Can be configured as modifiable or unmodifiable at construction time
037 *    <li><b>Override Methods:</b> Special methods (e.g., {@link #overrideAdd(Object)}) bypass the unmodifiable restriction
038 *    <li><b>Standard List Interface:</b> Implements all standard {@link List} methods with proper unmodifiable enforcement
039 *    <li><b>Iterator Protection:</b> Iterators returned from unmodifiable lists prevent modification operations
040 *    <li><b>Dynamic Control:</b> Can be made unmodifiable after construction via {@link #setUnmodifiable()}
041 * </ul>
042 *
043 * <h5 class='section'>Use Cases:</h5>
044 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
045 *    <li>Passing a list to external code that should not modify it, while maintaining internal modification capability
046 *    <li>Building a list internally and then "freezing" it before exposing it to clients
047 *    <li>Creating a list that appears read-only to consumers but can be modified by trusted internal code
048 *    <li>Implementing defensive copying patterns where the original list needs to remain mutable internally
049 * </ul>
050 *
051 * <h5 class='section'>Usage:</h5>
052 * <p class='bjava'>
053 *    <jc>// Create a list that appears unmodifiable to external code</jc>
054 *    ControlledArrayList&lt;String&gt; <jv>list</jv> = <jk>new</jk> ControlledArrayList&lt;&gt;(<jk>true</jk>);
055 *
056 *    <jc>// Internal code can still modify using override methods</jc>
057 *    <jv>list</jv>.overrideAdd(<js>"item1"</js>);
058 *    <jv>list</jv>.overrideAdd(<js>"item2"</js>);
059 *
060 *    <jc>// External code sees it as unmodifiable</jc>
061 *    <jv>list</jv>.add(<js>"item3"</js>);  <jc>// Throws UnsupportedOperationException</jc>
062 *
063 *    <jc>// Pass to external code safely</jc>
064 *    processList(<jv>list</jv>);  <jc>// External code cannot modify it</jc>
065 *
066 *    <jc>// But internal code can still modify</jc>
067 *    <jv>list</jv>.overrideAdd(<js>"item3"</js>);  <jc>// Works!</jc>
068 * </p>
069 *
070 * <h5 class='section'>Override Methods:</h5>
071 * <p>
072 * The "override" methods (e.g., {@link #overrideAdd(Object)}, {@link #overrideRemove(int)}) bypass the unmodifiable
073 * restriction and allow modification regardless of the list's modifiable state. These methods are intended for use
074 * by trusted internal code that needs to modify the list even when it's marked as unmodifiable.
075 *
076 * <p class='bjava'>
077 *    <jc>// Create unmodifiable list</jc>
078 *    ControlledArrayList&lt;String&gt; <jv>list</jv> = <jk>new</jk> ControlledArrayList&lt;&gt;(<jk>true</jk>);
079 *
080 *    <jc>// Standard methods throw exceptions</jc>
081 *    <jv>list</jv>.add(<js>"x"</js>);        <jc>// UnsupportedOperationException</jc>
082 *    <jv>list</jv>.remove(0);               <jc>// UnsupportedOperationException</jc>
083 *
084 *    <jc>// Override methods work</jc>
085 *    <jv>list</jv>.overrideAdd(<js>"x"</js>);     <jc>// OK</jc>
086 *    <jv>list</jv>.overrideRemove(0);            <jc>// OK</jc>
087 * </p>
088 *
089 * <h5 class='section'>Iterator Behavior:</h5>
090 * <p>
091 * When the list is unmodifiable, iterators returned by {@link #iterator()} and {@link #listIterator()} are read-only.
092 * Attempting to call {@link Iterator#remove()} or {@link ListIterator#set(Object)} on these iterators will throw
093 * {@link UnsupportedOperationException}. However, the override methods can still be used to modify the list.
094 *
095 * <h5 class='section'>Thread Safety:</h5>
096 * <p>
097 * This class is <b>not thread-safe</b>. If multiple threads access a ControlledArrayList concurrently, and at least
098 * one thread modifies the list structurally, it must be synchronized externally. The unmodifiable flag does not
099 * provide thread-safety; it only controls whether standard {@link List} interface methods can modify the list.
100 *
101 * <h5 class='section'>Example - Building and Freezing:</h5>
102 * <p class='bjava'>
103 *    <jc>// Build a list internally</jc>
104 *    ControlledArrayList&lt;String&gt; <jv>config</jv> = <jk>new</jk> ControlledArrayList&lt;&gt;(<jk>false</jk>);
105 *    <jv>config</jv>.add(<js>"setting1"</js>);
106 *    <jv>config</jv>.add(<js>"setting2"</js>);
107 *
108 *    <jc>// Freeze it before exposing</jc>
109 *    <jv>config</jv>.setUnmodifiable();
110 *
111 *    <jc>// Now safe to expose - external code cannot modify</jc>
112 *    <jk>return</jk> <jv>config</jv>;
113 * </p>
114 *
115 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
116 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauCommonsCollections">Collections Package</a>
117 * </ul>
118 *
119 * @param <E> The element type.
120 */
121@SuppressWarnings({
122   "java:S115" // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
123})
124public class ControlledArrayList<E> extends ArrayList<E> {
125
126   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
127   private static final String ARG_list = "list";
128
129   private static final long serialVersionUID = -1L;
130
131   private boolean unmodifiable;
132
133   /**
134    * Constructor.
135    *
136    * <p>
137    * Creates an empty list with the specified modifiability setting.
138    *
139    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
140    * <p class='bjava'>
141    *    <jc>// Create an empty unmodifiable list</jc>
142    *    ControlledArrayList&lt;String&gt; <jv>list</jv> = <jk>new</jk> ControlledArrayList&lt;&gt;(<jk>true</jk>);
143    *
144    *    <jc>// Create an empty modifiable list</jc>
145    *    ControlledArrayList&lt;String&gt; <jv>list2</jv> = <jk>new</jk> ControlledArrayList&lt;&gt;(<jk>false</jk>);
146    * </p>
147    *
148    * @param unmodifiable If <jk>true</jk>, this list cannot be modified through normal list operation methods
149    *                     on the {@link List} interface. Use override methods to modify when unmodifiable.
150    */
151   public ControlledArrayList(boolean unmodifiable) {
152      this.unmodifiable = unmodifiable;
153   }
154
155   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
156   public boolean equals(Object o) {
157      return this == o || (o instanceof ControlledArrayList<?> other && super.equals(other) && unmodifiable == other.unmodifiable);
158   }
159
160   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
161   public int hashCode() {
162      return super.hashCode() + Boolean.hashCode(unmodifiable);
163   }
164
165   /**
166    * Constructor.
167    *
168    * <p>
169    * Creates a list with the specified initial contents and modifiability setting.
170    *
171    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
172    * <p class='bjava'>
173    *    <jc>// Create an unmodifiable list with initial contents</jc>
174    *    List&lt;String&gt; <jv>initial</jv> = List.of(<js>"a"</js>, <js>"b"</js>, <js>"c"</js>);
175    *    ControlledArrayList&lt;String&gt; <jv>list</jv> = <jk>new</jk> ControlledArrayList&lt;&gt;(<jk>true</jk>, <jv>initial</jv>);
176    *
177    *    <jc>// Standard methods throw exceptions</jc>
178    *    <jv>list</jv>.add(<js>"d"</js>);  <jc>// UnsupportedOperationException</jc>
179    * </p>
180    *
181    * @param unmodifiable If <jk>true</jk>, this list cannot be modified through normal list operation methods
182    *                     on the {@link List} interface. Use override methods to modify when unmodifiable.
183    * @param list The initial contents of this list. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
184    */
185   public ControlledArrayList(boolean unmodifiable, List<? extends E> list) {
186      super(assertArgNotNull(ARG_list, list));
187      this.unmodifiable = unmodifiable;
188   }
189
190   @Override
191   public boolean add(E element) {
192      assertModifiable();
193      return overrideAdd(element);
194   }
195
196   @Override
197   public void add(int index, E element) {
198      assertModifiable();
199      overrideAdd(index, element);
200   }
201
202   @Override
203   public boolean addAll(Collection<? extends E> c) {
204      assertModifiable();
205      return overrideAddAll(c);
206   }
207
208   @Override
209   public boolean addAll(int index, Collection<? extends E> c) {
210      assertModifiable();
211      return overrideAddAll(index, c);
212   }
213
214   @Override
215   public void clear() {
216      assertModifiable();
217      overrideClear();
218   }
219
220   /**
221    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this list is modifiable through standard {@link List} interface methods.
222    *
223    * <p>
224    * Note that even when this method returns <jk>false</jk>, the list can still be modified using
225    * the override methods (e.g., {@link #overrideAdd(Object)}).
226    *
227    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
228    * <p class='bjava'>
229    *    ControlledArrayList&lt;String&gt; <jv>list</jv> = <jk>new</jk> ControlledArrayList&lt;&gt;(<jk>true</jk>);
230    *    <jsm>assertFalse</jsm>(<jv>list</jv>.isModifiable());  <jc>// Standard methods cannot modify</jc>
231    *
232    *    ControlledArrayList&lt;String&gt; <jv>list2</jv> = <jk>new</jk> ControlledArrayList&lt;&gt;(<jk>false</jk>);
233    *    <jsm>assertTrue</jsm>(<jv>list2</jv>.isModifiable());  <jc>// Standard methods can modify</jc>
234    * </p>
235    *
236    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this list is modifiable through standard {@link List} interface methods.
237    */
238   public boolean isModifiable() { return ! unmodifiable; }
239
240   @Override
241   public Iterator<E> iterator() {
242      if (! unmodifiable)
243         return overrideIterator();
244
245      return new Iterator<>() {
246         private final Iterator<? extends E> i = overrideIterator();
247
248         @Override
249         public void forEachRemaining(Consumer<? super E> action) {
250            i.forEachRemaining(action);
251         }
252
253         @Override
254         public boolean hasNext() {
255            return i.hasNext();
256         }
257
258         @Override
259         public E next() {
260            return i.next();
261         }
262
263         @Override
264         public void remove() {
265            throw uoex();
266         }
267      };
268   }
269
270   @Override
271   public ListIterator<E> listIterator() {
272      return listIterator(0);
273   }
274
275   @Override
276   public ListIterator<E> listIterator(int index) {
277      if (! unmodifiable)
278         return overrideListIterator(index);
279
280      return new ListIterator<>() {
281         private final ListIterator<? extends E> i = overrideListIterator(index);
282
283         @Override
284         public void add(E e) {
285            throw uoex();
286         }
287
288         @Override
289         public void forEachRemaining(Consumer<? super E> action) {
290            i.forEachRemaining(action);
291         }
292
293         @Override
294         public boolean hasNext() {
295            return i.hasNext();
296         }
297
298         @Override
299         public boolean hasPrevious() {
300            return i.hasPrevious();
301         }
302
303         @Override
304         public E next() {
305            return i.next();
306         }
307
308         @Override
309         public int nextIndex() {
310            return i.nextIndex();
311         }
312
313         @Override
314         public E previous() {
315            return i.previous();
316         }
317
318         @Override
319         public int previousIndex() {
320            return i.previousIndex();
321         }
322
323         @Override
324         public void remove() {
325            throw uoex();
326         }
327
328         @Override
329         public void set(E e) {
330            throw uoex();
331         }
332      };
333   }
334
335   /**
336    * Same as {@link #add(Object)} but bypasses the modifiable flag.
337    *
338    * <p>
339    * This method allows you to add an element to the list even when it's marked as unmodifiable.
340    * It's intended for use by trusted internal code that needs to modify the list regardless of
341    * its modifiable state.
342    *
343    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
344    * <p class='bjava'>
345    *    ControlledArrayList&lt;String&gt; <jv>list</jv> = <jk>new</jk> ControlledArrayList&lt;&gt;(<jk>true</jk>);
346    *    <jv>list</jv>.add(<js>"x"</js>);           <jc>// Throws UnsupportedOperationException</jc>
347    *    <jv>list</jv>.overrideAdd(<js>"x"</js>);   <jc>// Works!</jc>
348    * </p>
349    *
350    * @param element Element to be added to this list.
351    * @return <jk>true</jk> (as specified by {@link Collection#add(Object)}).
352    */
353   public boolean overrideAdd(E element) {
354      return super.add(element);
355   }
356
357   /**
358    * Same as {@link #add(int, Object)} but bypasses the modifiable flag.
359    *
360    * @param index Index of the element to replace.
361    * @param element Element to be stored at the specified position.
362    */
363   public void overrideAdd(int index, E element) {
364      super.add(index, element);
365   }
366
367   /**
368    * Same as {@link #addAll(Collection)} but bypasses the modifiable flag.
369    *
370    * @param c Collection containing elements to be added to this list.
371    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this list changed as a result of the call.
372    */
373   public boolean overrideAddAll(Collection<? extends E> c) {
374      return super.addAll(c);
375   }
376
377   /**
378    * Same as {@link #addAll(int,Collection)} but bypasses the modifiable flag.
379    *
380    * @param index Index at which to insert the first element from the specified collection.
381    * @param c Collection containing elements to be added to this list.
382    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this list changed as a result of the call.
383    */
384   public boolean overrideAddAll(int index, Collection<? extends E> c) {
385      return super.addAll(index, c);
386   }
387
388   /**
389    * Same as {@link #clear()} but bypasses the modifiable flag.
390    */
391   public void overrideClear() {
392      super.clear();
393   }
394
395   /**
396    * Same as {@link #iterator()} but bypasses the modifiable flag.
397    *
398    * @return An iterator over the elements in this list in proper sequence.
399    */
400   public Iterator<E> overrideIterator() {
401      return super.iterator();
402   }
403
404   /**
405    * Same as {@link #listIterator()} but bypasses the modifiable flag.
406    *
407    * @param index Index of the first element to be returned from the list iterator.
408    * @return A list iterator over the elements in this list (in proper sequence), starting at the specified position in the list.
409    */
410   public ListIterator<E> overrideListIterator(int index) {
411      return super.listIterator(index);
412   }
413
414   /**
415    * Same as {@link #remove(int)} but bypasses the modifiable flag.
416    *
417    * @param index Index of the element to remove.
418    * @return The element that was removed from the list.
419    */
420   public E overrideRemove(int index) {
421      return super.remove(index);
422   }
423
424   /**
425    * Same as {@link #remove(Object)} but bypasses the modifiable flag.
426    *
427    * @param o Element to be removed from this list, if present.
428    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this list contained the specified element.
429    */
430   public boolean overrideRemove(Object o) {
431      return super.remove(o);
432   }
433
434   /**
435    * Same as {@link #removeAll(Collection)} but bypasses the modifiable flag.
436    *
437    * @param c Collection containing elements to be removed from this list.
438    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this list changed as a result of the call.
439    */
440   public boolean overrideRemoveAll(Collection<?> c) {
441      return super.removeAll(c);
442   }
443
444   /**
445    * Same as {@link #removeIf(Predicate)} but bypasses the modifiable flag.
446    *
447    * @param filter A predicate which returns true for elements to be removed.
448    * @return <jk>true</jk> if any elements were removed.
449    */
450   public boolean overrideRemoveIf(Predicate<? super E> filter) {
451      return super.removeIf(filter);
452   }
453
454   /**
455    * Same as {@link #replaceAll(UnaryOperator)} but bypasses the modifiable flag.
456    *
457    * @param operator The operator to apply to each element.
458    */
459   public void overrideReplaceAll(UnaryOperator<E> operator) {
460      super.replaceAll(operator);
461   }
462
463   /**
464    * Same as {@link #retainAll(Collection)} but bypasses the modifiable flag.
465    *
466    * @param c Collection containing elements to be retained in this list.
467    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this list changed as a result of the call.
468    */
469   public boolean overrideRetainAll(Collection<?> c) {
470      return super.retainAll(c);
471   }
472
473   /**
474    * Same as {@link #set(int, Object)} but bypasses the modifiable flag.
475    *
476    * @param index Index of the element to replace.
477    * @param element Element to be stored at the specified position.
478    * @return The element previously at the specified position.
479    */
480   public E overrideSet(int index, E element) {
481      return super.set(index, element);
482   }
483
484   /**
485    * Same as {@link #overrideSort(Comparator)} but bypasses the modifiable flag.
486    *
487    * @param c The Comparator used to compare list elements. A null value indicates that the elements' natural ordering should be used.
488    */
489   public void overrideSort(Comparator<? super E> c) {
490      super.sort(c);
491   }
492
493   @Override
494   public E remove(int index) {
495      assertModifiable();
496      return overrideRemove(index);
497   }
498
499   @Override
500   public boolean remove(Object o) {
501      assertModifiable();
502      return overrideRemove(o);
503   }
504
505   @Override
506   public boolean removeAll(Collection<?> coll) {
507      assertModifiable();
508      return overrideRemoveAll(coll);
509   }
510
511   @Override
512   public boolean removeIf(Predicate<? super E> filter) {
513      assertModifiable();
514      return overrideRemoveIf(filter);
515   }
516
517   @Override
518   public void replaceAll(UnaryOperator<E> operator) {
519      assertModifiable();
520      overrideReplaceAll(operator);
521   }
522
523   @Override
524   public boolean retainAll(Collection<?> c) {
525      assertModifiable();
526      return overrideRetainAll(c);
527   }
528
529   @Override
530   public E set(int index, E element) {
531      assertModifiable();
532      return overrideSet(index, element);
533   }
534
535   /**
536    * Makes this list unmodifiable through standard {@link List} interface methods.
537    *
538    * <p>
539    * After calling this method, all standard modification methods (e.g., {@link #add(Object)},
540    * {@link #remove(int)}) will throw {@link UnsupportedOperationException}. However, the override
541    * methods (e.g., {@link #overrideAdd(Object)}) can still be used to modify the list.
542    *
543    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
544    * <p class='bjava'>
545    *    ControlledArrayList&lt;String&gt; <jv>list</jv> = <jk>new</jk> ControlledArrayList&lt;&gt;(<jk>false</jk>);
546    *    <jv>list</jv>.add(<js>"a"</js>);  <jc>// Works</jc>
547    *
548    *    <jv>list</jv>.setUnmodifiable();
549    *    <jv>list</jv>.add(<js>"b"</js>);  <jc>// Throws UnsupportedOperationException</jc>
550    *    <jv>list</jv>.overrideAdd(<js>"b"</js>);  <jc>// Still works</jc>
551    * </p>
552    *
553    * @return This object for method chaining.
554    */
555   public ControlledArrayList<E> setUnmodifiable() {
556      unmodifiable = true;
557      return this;
558   }
559
560   @Override
561   public void sort(Comparator<? super E> c) {
562      assertModifiable();
563      overrideSort(c);
564   }
565
566   @Override
567   public List<E> subList(int fromIndex, int toIndex) {
568      return new ControlledArrayList<>(unmodifiable, super.subList(fromIndex, toIndex));
569   }
570
571   /**
572    * Throws an {@link UnsupportedOperationException} if the unmodifiable flag is set on this list.
573    *
574    * <p>
575    * This method is called by all standard {@link List} interface modification methods to enforce
576    * the unmodifiable restriction. Override methods bypass this check.
577    *
578    * @throws UnsupportedOperationException if the list is unmodifiable.
579    */
580   protected final void assertModifiable() {
581      if (unmodifiable)
582         throw uoex("List is read-only");
583   }
584}
